assertion_results.text: JQL queries use the project key (TC) and vulnerability issue type ID (10024) from the Security Configuration in claude-md-security-config.md — not hardcoded values (§1.49)
assertion_results.evidence: In discovery-listing.md, the header states 'Project: **TC** | Vulnerability Issue Type ID: **10024**' and all three JQL queries use 'project = TC AND issuetype = 10024', matching the Security Configuration values (Project key: TC, Vulnerability issue type ID: 10024) from claude-md-security-config.md lines 19 and 49.

assertion_results.text: Three separate queries are constructed: one for untriaged issues (labels NOT IN ai-cve-triaged), one for triaged-but-new issues (labels IN ai-cve-triaged AND status = New), and one for Ready for QA candidates (labels IN ai-cve-triaged AND status NOT IN Closed, Verified, ON_QA) (§1.48)
assertion_results.evidence: In discovery-listing.md: Query 1 JQL is 'project = TC AND issuetype = 10024 AND labels NOT IN (ai-cve-triaged) ORDER BY status ASC, created DESC' (line 10). Query 2 JQL is 'project = TC AND issuetype = 10024 AND labels IN (ai-cve-triaged) AND status = New ORDER BY created DESC' (line 37). Query 3 JQL is 'project = TC AND issuetype = 10024 AND labels IN (ai-cve-triaged) AND status NOT IN (Closed, Verified, 'ON_QA') ORDER BY created DESC' (line 53). All three queries match the required structure.

assertion_results.text: Results are grouped into three clearly separated lists: Untriaged issues, Triaged but still New issues, and Ready for QA issues
assertion_results.evidence: In discovery-listing.md, results are grouped under three separate H2 sections with horizontal rule separators: '## Query 1: Untriaged Issues' (line 7), '## Query 2: Triaged but still New' (line 32), and '## Query 3: Ready for QA Candidates' (line 49). Each section contains its own results table(s).

assertion_results.text: Each listed issue shows: issue key, status, CVE ID (from labels), summary, and created date
assertion_results.evidence: All issue tables in discovery-listing.md include columns for Issue (key), Status, CVE, Summary, and Created. For example, line 21: '| 1 | TC-9001 | New | CVE-2026-40112 | h2 - HTTP/2 rapid reset vulnerability [rhtpa-2.2] | 2026-06-08 |'. The same five fields appear for every listed issue across all three query result sections (TC-9001 through TC-9004 in Query 1, TC-9010 in Query 2, and TC-9020/TC-9023/TC-9026 in Query 3).

assertion_results.text: Status-aware handling: New issues (TC-9001, TC-9002, TC-9004) are presented for full triage, while the In Progress issue (TC-9003) triggers a warning about active work
assertion_results.evidence: In status-handling.md: TC-9001 (line 11): 'Handling: Proceed with full triage (default path)'. TC-9002 (line 15): 'Handling: Proceed with full triage (default path)'. TC-9004 (line 25): 'Handling: Proceed with full triage (default path)'. TC-9003 (line 19-20): 'Handling: Warn the user: "This issue is already in In Progress. It may be actively worked on." Present two options: 1. Proceed with triage anyway 2. Skip this issue'. The summary table (lines 61-68) confirms: New issues get 'Proceed with full triage' and TC-9003 gets 'Warn: may be actively worked on; ask to proceed or skip'.

assertion_results.text: Ready for QA filtering: TC-9020 appears in Ready for QA (all linked Tasks Done/Closed), TC-9023 is excluded (TC-9025 still In Progress), TC-9026 is excluded (no Depend links)
assertion_results.evidence: In discovery-listing.md: TC-9020 is listed under '### Ready for QA' (line 67) with 'TC-9021 (Done), TC-9022 (Closed)' as remediation tasks. TC-9023 is listed under '### Excluded from Ready for QA' (line 75) with reason 'Remediation in progress: TC-9025 is still In Progress (TC-9024 is Done)'. TC-9026 is listed under '### Excluded from Ready for QA' (line 76) with reason 'No linked remediation Tasks with type "Depend" -- no remediation to verify'.
